name: Patout's Boiled Crawfish
recipeInstructions:
1. Fill a large stockpot (approximately 40 quarts) about one-quarter full with water.
2. Add the salt and all three peppers (white, red, and black) to the water. Bring to a boil.
3. Add the whole onions, corn, and potatoes (if using a cloth sack, they'll be easy to remove later). Return to a boil.
4. Cover, lower the heat to medium, and cook for 8 minutes.
5. Add the crawfish; cover and raise the heat to high.
6. When steam begins to escape from under the lid, cook for an additional 7 minutes.
7. Remove from heat and let sit for 4 minutes before opening the lid.
8. Transfer the cooked onions, corn, and potatoes to a bowl and drain the crawfish.
9. Place the crawfish in a large insulated container (a cooler or thick waterproof box works well).
10. Sprinkle the remaining salt over the crawfish and mix well to coat.
11. Cover and let sit for an additional 7 minutes before serving.
12. Serve immediately with the vegetables on a large, paper-covered table. When everyone is finished, those who are "peeling for the house" can take any remaining tails for cold crawfish cocktail or salad, or for frying later.
Serves 8-10
Note: Most of the salt is added after cooking begins to prevent the flesh from adhering to the shell.
